text: coffee shops that play music too loud at 9am should be tried at The Hague. i paid $6 for oat milk silence, not a club remix of Coldplay.
the snooze button is humanity's greatest invention. not the internet. not penicillin. the snooze button. it gave us the gift of organized denial.
dating in your 30s is just two people comparing bedtimes. "i'm asleep by 10." "same." congrats you're soulmates.
airports are the only place where beer at 7am is fine, a $19 sandwich is normal, and crying in public is expected. lawless paradise.
the fridge when you work from home is the final boss. it's not even hunger. it's the fridge going "hey. hey. what if you were hungry tho."
"let's circle back" is corporate for "i'm gonna forget this and i need you to also forget this." beautiful invention honestly.
hot take: the best meal of your life was never at a nice restaurant. it was 2am, street cart, questionable meat, best friends. michelin could never.
grocery shopping alone is elite self-care and i will die on this hill. no kids. no partner judging your cart. just you and the cheese aisle in peace.
the bravest thing you can do in modern society is send food back at a restaurant. i have eaten things i'm allergic to because confrontation at dinner feels illegal.
friend groups have exactly one person who's scary good at picking restaurants. you follow them blindly. they say "trust me" and suddenly you're in a basement eating life-changing ramen.
normalize canceling plans with no explanation. "can't make it." "cool." done. no fake cough. no dead grandma. just vibes and mutual respect.
every generation thinks they invented being tired. boomers tired. gen x tired. millennials tired. gen z tired. we are a species united by exhaustion.
"i'll sleep when i'm dead" — said by someone who has clearly never experienced a 20-minute sunday nap. that's not sleep. that's enlightenment.
the real test of friendship is not your wedding. it's moving day. someone carries your couch up three flights of stairs? that's a blood oath. protect that human.
people who reply to emails in under 2 minutes genuinely scare me. where are you. what is your life. do you live inside Outlook.
adulthood is just googling stuff you feel like you should already know. "how to do taxes." "is this mole normal." "what temp to wash towels." we're all winging it.
small talk is humanity's worst UX. two people who both hate small talk, doing small talk, both wishing they could skip to the real conversation. someone fix this.
movie theaters with assigned seating online are doing god's work. the old walk-in-and-pray system was pure anarchy. never going back.
the older i get the more i realize "having your life together" just means better folders. nobody is organized. some people just hide the chaos more efficiently.
real luxury isn't a car or a house. it's a saturday with nothing to do and zero guilt about it. that's the endgame. a perfectly empty calendar.
bookstores are the only place where you buy 5 things you didn't plan to and feel zero regret. target does this too but with more financial damage and candles.
the universal human experience is pretending you've seen a movie everyone's discussing. "oh yeah great film." you haven't seen it. you never will. you'll still defend it.
cooking for one is either empowering or devastating depending entirely on whether it was your idea. same pasta. very different energy.
we all just agreed to wear uncomfortable shoes to formal events and nobody questions it. society is a series of bad decisions we inherited and never patched.
i trust people who return shopping carts more than people with great LinkedIn profiles. no one's watching at the cart return. that's pure unfiltered character.
the fastest way to ruin a sunday is remembering something due monday. your brain just drops "oh btw quarterly report" at 6pm like a psychopath.
hot take: the best part of any vacation is the first night back in your own bed. travel feeds your soul but your mattress feeds your spine. spine wins.
gym is just adult daycare with squat racks. we go there to run from our problems while pretending we're running toward solutions. but hey, the problems look toned now.
cities with bad public transit have no right being expensive. you want $3,000 rent AND i need three ubers to get a sandwich? pick a struggle.
the person who invented "per my last email" understood violence on a level most of us will never reach. weaponized politeness. absolute art form.
